description: Validate the merged CEAC DS-160 runtime on real execution paths. Prove that the merged CAPTCHA solver + full-page-fill implementation actually works from the live worker entrypoint on main: the smoke path must reach a post-CAPTCHA surface, a real prefill run must no longer die as ds160_blocked at bootstrap, and persisted telemetry/result payloads must truthfully record solve attempts plus filled/skipped sections.

scopeContract.truthStatement: Code-complete CEAC automation is not enough. The merged main-branch runtime must be validated through the live worker and smoke paths, with truthful persisted outcomes that distinguish handoff-ready success, runtime failure, and genuine anti-bot/manual blockage.
scopeContract.rejectedAssumption: A passing typecheck or completed implementation PRD does NOT prove the CEAC worker is operational on main. The next pass must verify real runtime behavior, not just module existence or static code structure.
scopeContract.designPrinciple: runtime-truth-over-static-completeness: prefer live-path validation, persisted evidence, and explicit operator-visible outcomes over inferred success from code shape alone.
scopeContract.integrationNotes: Use the merged main branch only. Preserve the stop-at-sign contract. Do not add new bypass behavior for unsupported anti-bot systems. Use existing TWOCAPTCHA_API_KEY env wiring; never commit secrets.

userStories.title: Pin the CEAC runtime-validation scope and truth contract on main
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the repo PRD to explicitly shift from implementation-complete to runtime-validated on merged main, so Ralph optimizes for live-path proof instead of more speculative feature work.
acceptanceCriteria: prd.json on main contains US-027 through US-031 for the CEAC runtime-validation run
branchName is main
scopeContract explicitly states that runtime validation on merged main is now the goal
scopeContract explicitly rejects treating typecheck/implementation completion as live proof
integrationNotes preserve stop-at-sign and env-only secret handling
userStories.notes: Scope anchor for the next Ralph pass. The implementation branch is already merged; this run is about proving runtime truth.

userStories.title: Prove the merged smoke path reaches a post-CAPTCHA CEAC surface
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the CEAC smoke path on merged main to exercise the new --solve-captcha flow and report whether it reaches a real post-CAPTCHA surface, so we know the bootstrap path works beyond static code checks.
acceptanceCriteria: The smoke path on main is runnable with --solve-captcha from the merged repo state
The smoke result clearly reports whether it reached a post-CAPTCHA CEAC surface, remained blocked, or failed
If required, smoke diagnostics are tightened so the reached surface is explicit in structured output
Typecheck passes for any code/documentation changes made
userStories.notes: This story is about truthful smoke-path verification and operator-facing runtime evidence, not expanding feature scope.

userStories.title: Validate one real DS-160 prefill run on the merged worker path
userStories.description: As an operator, I need one real main-branch DS-160 worker run to use the merged CEAC bootstrap/orchestrator path and no longer die at startup as ds160_blocked solely because the start-page image CAPTCHA exists.
acceptanceCriteria: The live worker entrypoint on main is exercised on a real DS-160 prefill attempt
The run uses the merged CEAC bootstrap/orchestrator path rather than the legacy prototype path
If the run cannot complete, the failure mode is classified truthfully with concrete evidence (for example bootstrap runtime failure vs genuine anti-bot/manual gate)
Typecheck passes for any code changes made to make the worker path truthful/observable
userStories.notes: This is the first real worker-path proof story. It is acceptable to surface a truthful blocker if CEAC or runtime conditions prevent full success.

userStories.title: Verify persisted CEAC result payload telemetry on merged main
userStories.description: As an operator, I need persisted ceac_result_payload data from the merged runtime to record what actually happened — including CAPTCHA solve attempts and section fill/skip state — so runtime outcomes can be trusted after the run.
acceptanceCriteria: A merged-main runtime attempt produces or inspects a ceac_result_payload example with CAPTCHA solve telemetry present when applicable
The payload clearly records filled vs skipped sections for the orchestrator
The payload classification aligns with the real runtime outcome (handoff_ready, runtime failure, or blocked/manual-required)
If payload gaps are found, code is updated so persisted results reflect the actual runtime truthfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the persistence/truthfulness story for the merged runtime pass.

userStories.title: Document the merged-main CEAC runtime validation procedure and next blocker
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the repo docs and progress log to state exactly how to rerun the merged-main CEAC validation flow and what blocker or success state remains afterward, so future runs do not repeat today’s ambiguity.
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation describes how to run the smoke validation and the real worker-path check from merged main
Documentation states the observed outcome from this validation pass and the next concrete blocker or next action
progress.txt is updated with the runtime-validation results and learnings
Typecheck passes for any code/doc-related changes made
userStories.notes: Close the loop with operator-usable documentation grounded in the actual validation run.
